You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: ce93a6db).

## Issue to Resolve

No spacing around colon in annotation
Spacing should not be allowed around the colon in annotation and annotation entries regardless of the annotation target

```
@file:JvmName("Foo") // allowed
@file :JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file: JvmName("Foo") // disallowed
@file : JvmName("Foo") // disallowed

@set:[Inject VisibleForTesting] // allowed
@set :[Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set: [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
@set : [Inject VisibleForTesting] // disallowed
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 16→
 17→### Changed
 18→
 19→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 20→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 21→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 22→
 23→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 24→
 25→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 26→
 27→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 28→
 29→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 30→
 31→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 32→
 33→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 34→
 35→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 36→
 37→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 38→
 39→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 40→
 41→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 42→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 43→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 44→
 45→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 46→
 47→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 48→
 49→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 50→
 51→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 52→
 53→### Added
 54→
 55→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 56→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 57→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 58→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 59→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 60→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 61→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 62→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 63→
 64→### Removed
 65→
 66→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 67→
 68→### Fixed
 69→
 70→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 71→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 72→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 73→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 74→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 75→* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 76→
 77→### Changed
 78→
 79→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. content.text:
The issue was that `SpacingAroundColonRule` did not enforce "no spacing" around colons in annotations, instead it was returning early for annotation elements. This allowed code like `@file : JvmName("Foo")` to pass linting, which is incorrect.

I have implemented the fix by:
1.  Modifying `SpacingAroundColonRule.kt` to check for spacing around colons when the colon is a direct child of `ANNOTATION` or `ANNOTATION_ENTRY`.
2.  If spacing is found (either before or after), a lint violation "Unexpected spacing around \":\"" is emitted.
3.  The auto-correct logic removes the unexpected whitespace.
4.  The logic ensures that nested colons (e.g. in object literals inside annotations) are not affected, as they are not direct children of `ANNOTATION` or `ANNOTATION_ENTRY`.

I have added a test case in `SpacingAroundColonAnnotationTest.kt` (which I created and verified, then deleted) to reproduce the issue and verify the fix. I also ran the existing `SpacingAroundColonRuleTest` to ensure no regressions were introduced.

I also updated `CHANGELOG.md` to document the fix.

Ref: https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093

Files modified:
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundColonRule.kt`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

Tests passed:
- `SpacingAroundColonAnnotationTest` (New test case)
- `SpacingAroundColonRuleTest` (Existing tests)

 23→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 24→
 25→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 26→
 27→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 28→
 29→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 30→
 31→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 32→
 33→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 34→
 35→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 36→
 37→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 38→
 39→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 40→
 41→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 42→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 43→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 44→
 45→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 46→
 47→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 48→
 49→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 50→
 51→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 52→
 53→### Added
 54→
 55→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 56→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 57→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 58→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 59→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 60→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 61→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 62→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 63→
 64→### Removed
 65→
 66→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 67→
 68→### Fixed
 69→
 70→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 71→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
